seoTitle: How to File Taxes Without a W-2 on TurboTax
metaDescription: Self-employed or missing a W-2? Here's how to accurately report your income and file your taxes in TurboTax without a traditional W-2 form.
intro: Not everyone gets a traditional W-2, especially freelancers and people with alternative income sources, but TurboTax can still handle your filing accurately. This guide covers gathering the right documentation and inputting your income correctly without a W-2. It's designed for anyone navigating self-employment or non-traditional work arrangements at tax time.

steps.heading: Gather necessary documentation
steps.body: Collect the records of your income, such as 1099 forms or your own transaction records, in place of a W-2.

steps.heading: Follow TurboTax's step-by-step instructions
steps.body: Use TurboTax's guided prompts to enter your income accurately based on the documentation you have available.

steps.heading: Avoid common pitfalls during preparation
steps.body: Watch for underreported income or missed deductions, which are common issues for filers without a standard W-2.

steps.heading: Maximize deductions and credits
steps.body: Review the deductions available to self-employed filers to make sure you're capturing everything you're eligible for.

faq.question: What documents do I need if I don't have a W-2?
faq.answer: You'll typically use 1099 forms, bank records, or your own income tracking depending on how you earned money throughout the year.

faq.question: Can freelancers file without any official income forms?
faq.answer: Yes, TurboTax supports entering self-reported income for freelancers and self-employed individuals who may not receive formal tax forms for every income source.

faq.question: What's the biggest mistake people make filing without a W-2?
faq.answer: A common pitfall is underreporting income or missing deductions specific to self-employment, so careful documentation review helps avoid errors.

faq.question: Does TurboTax help maximize deductions for self-employed filers?
faq.answer: Yes, TurboTax is built to walk self-employed users through deductions and credits relevant to their income type as part of the guided filing process.
